# Striatum: Recovery

Use this skill when a run looks stuck, a job is sitting in
`blocked`, or `doctor` is reporting structured problems.

## When to invoke

- A job has been in `running` longer than its lease window.
- A blocker is open and you do not know how to clear it.
- `striatum status` reports `next_actions` like
  `recover_orphan_supervisor`,
  `clear_human_checkpoint`, `requeue_stale_review`.

## Authoritative reference

- `striatum status` — Read run/job/blocker state.
- `striatum why` — Explain why a job is in its current state.
- `striatum doctor --verbose` — Surface structured consistency problems.
- `striatum recovery stale-leases` — Lazy-expire and report stale leases.
- `striatum recovery requeue-stale` — Requeue an expired non-repo-write job.
- `striatum recovery process-reconcile` — Reconcile externally-killed processes.
- `striatum recovery resume` — Resolve remediated process-adapter blockers.
- `striatum checkpoint resolve` — Resolve a `human_checkpoint` blocker.
- `striatum dashboard --once` — Render a single dashboard frame for scripts.

Use the local operator UI or daemon MCP recovery methods for live recovery.
`striatum <verb> --help` documents CLI compatibility fallbacks for the same
daemon methods.

## Common patterns

```json
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"recovery.stale_leases","repository_id":"<repository-id>","arguments":{"run_id":"<run-id>"}}}
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"recovery.requeue_stale","repository_id":"<repository-id>","arguments":{"run_id":"<run-id>","job_id":"<job-id>"}}}
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"recovery.process_reconcile","repository_id":"<repository-id>","arguments":{"run_id":"<run-id>"}}}
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"recovery.resume","repository_id":"<repository-id>","arguments":{"blocker_id":"<blocker-id>"}}}
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"checkpoint.resolve","repository_id":"<repository-id>","arguments":{"blocker_id":"<blocker-id>","action":"continue"}}}
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"checkpoint.resolve","repository_id":"<repository-id>","arguments":{"blocker_id":"<blocker-id>","action":"override","decision_id":"<decision-id>"}}}
```

`checkpoint.resolve` action `override` is for `revision_routing`
checkpoints only: it accepts the `needs_revision` verdict as superseded
by an accepting run-level decision and makes the downstream gate reachable
without re-running the review. It requires `decision_id`.

`recovery requeue-stale` refuses repo-write jobs (D036). For
abandoned repo-write work, inspect with `recovery stale-leases
For remediated process-adapter blockers, use `recovery resume`; if a
review verdict is missing, resume first and then record the verdict normally.

## Where to look next

- **`striatum-claim-loop`** — once the blocker is clear, the loop
  resumes.

## What not to do

- Do not bypass the daemon (the CLI is the only client; the daemon is the single writer per D094 / RFC 0043); never open Postgres directly, and do not treat `.striatum/` scratch files as workflow state.
- Do not treat marker files, tmux panes, or terminal output as workflow state.
- Do not advance state by printing phrases; advance by calling the CLI verbs in your work packet.
- Do not capture stdout/stderr transcripts; D028 keeps them off by default.
- Do not derive bylines from job titles; use the byline supplied in your work packet.
- Do not parse a supervisor's own output for workflow state; supervisors send DEVNULL.
